message:
feat(perf): enforce FAIL status for CI-enforced budgets with missing measurement data

Update performance budget reporting policy: CI-enforced budgets that lack
measurement data now report FAIL (with failure_reason: missing_measurement_data)
instead of the previous NO_DATA status. This makes the budget system stricter
by treating missing data as a failure condition rather than an informational gap.

Concrete changes across all three report files:
- 7 CI-enforced budgets promoted from NO_DATA → FAIL: startup_version_p95,
  ext_cold_load_simple_p95, tool_call_latency_p99, tool_call_throughput_min,
  policy_eval_p99, binary_size_release, protocol_parse_p99
- Each FAIL event now includes a "failure_reason":"missing_measurement_data" field
  in budget_events.jsonl for programmatic triage
- Summary counters updated: ci_fail 0→7, ci_no_data 7→0, fail 0→7, no_data 10→3
- idle_memory_rss actual value updated from 4.59 MB → 3.65 MB (measurement refresh)
- Report regeneration timestamp updated

This ensures CI gates cannot silently pass when perf data collection is broken.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
